title: Data from: Beyond the colony-forming-unit: Rapid bacterial evaluation in Osteomyelitis
author: Yang, Dongqing
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: Examination of bacteria/host cell interactions is important for understanding the aetiology of many infectious diseases. The colony-forming-unit (CFU) has been the standard for quantifying bacterial burden for the past century, however, this suffers from low sensitivity and is dependent on bacterial culturability in vitro. Our data demonstrate the discrepancy between the CFU and bacterial genome copy number in an osteomyelitis-relevant co-culture system and we confirm diagnosis and quantify bacterial load in clinical bone specimens. This study provides insight into improving the quantification of bacterial burden in such cases.
steps_raw: Examination of bacteria/host cell interactions is important for understanding the aetiology of many infectious diseases. This study provides insight into improving the quantification of bacterial burden in such cases.
